PPI - Intraday post (14/Jan)

Weekly post:

January OpEx week quant breakdown | Weekly post (12-16/Jan) - FREE

Last weekly post upside target was hit.

The weekly post is entirely free. It contains the key levels, and expected dynamics and my derivation of sentiment from the vol markets.

PPI’was made’ dovish on near-term inflation momentum (core m/m = 0 vs +0.2) but hawkish on the 12‑month inflation backdrop (y/y = 3% vs 2.7).Most desks react fastest to core m/m because it’s “current momentum” and feeds forward inflation expectations.If yields fall and Nasdaq leads that means, market is weighting core m/m. If yields rise back and Dow/value holds better that means market is rotating toward “y/y stickiness” framing. Inflation impulse (near-term) is cooling, but inflation backdrop (12‑mo) is sticky. Momentum remains dovish (CPI core cooler vs exp; PPI core m/m even cooler vs exp), but the narrative gets noisier.Choppiness, and and more rotational than smooth trend is expected further.

Let’s look at the daily positioning…

INTRADAY POST Coding today’s positioning:

Net negative speed profile, downside is supported, upside squeeze is priced in (upside vvol is barely sold), but more magnetic effects from the downside there. But let’s where…

Centroid is at 6896.71 Very important support into the PM.

Downside pivot is at 6883.51Downside target is 6859.02Here, 6870 is pinning locally. Otherside a reversion back towards the centroid and then pin 6870 is expected.

Upside pivot is at 6937.92Upside target is 6983.20Here market prices a reversion, potentially back to the pivot and pin there (or at 6925.

Chop is expected, nothing euphoric.

OptionsDepth Heatmap
